verbatim_quote: The north remembers, Lord Davos. The north remembers, and the mummer's farce is almost done. My son is home.
note: Manderly's explicit statement about remembering the Red Wedding betrayal

verbatim_quote: The lad is ironborn, so he thought it best not to show himself. He listened. The six did not linger long amongst the ruins of Winterfell. Four went one way, two another. Wex stole after the two, a woman and a boy. He must have stayed downwind, so the wolf would not catch his scent.
note: Wex's eyewitness testimony that a woman and a boy escaped Winterfell—evidence Bran and Rickon are alive, which Robett Glover knows through this conversation

verbatim_quote: Harwin had the door when Ned returned to the Tower of the Hand.
note: Establishes Harwin as one of Ned's household guard/trusted servants

verbatim_quote: Only six Winterfell men remained of the twenty her father had sent west with Beric Dondarrion, Harwin told her, and they were scattered.
note: Harwin was one of Ned's men sent with Beric Dondarrion; confirms involvement with the Brotherhood Without Banners

verbatim_quote: She is, said Thoros of Myr. The Freys slashed her throat from ear to ear. When we found her by the river she was three days dead. Harwin begged me to give her the kiss of life, but it had been too long. I would not do it, so Lord Beric put his lips to hers instead, and the flame of life passed from him to her. And . . . she rose. May the Lord of Light protect us. She rose.
note: Harwin with Lady Stoneheart's Brotherhood group; Harwin specifically pleaded to resurrect her

verbatim_quote: That was not us. I told you.
note: Rowan denies killing the boy (Little Walder); followed by admission of Yellow Dick murder at line 115

verbatim_quote: I've noticed, said the three-eyed crow. It took to the air, flapping its wings in his face, slowing him, blinding him.
note: Three-eyed crow appearing in Bran's flying dream; opens his third eye to magical sight

Tried grep patterns: 'Some men can skin-change', 'grim magic', 'skin-change' in asos-davos-04.md and surrounding chapters; quote does not appear verbatim in corpus. May be from show-only content or interpretive paraphrase rather than direct quote.

verbatim_quote: Bran looked down, and felt his insides turn to water. The ground was rushing up at him now. The whole world was spread out below him, a tapestry of white and brown and green. He could see everything so clearly that for a moment he forgot to be afraid. He could see the whole realm, and everyone in it.
note: Bran's flying dream showing the whole world spread below; mirrors the video's claim about Euron's dream vision

verbatim_quote: What feeds a dragon's fire? All Valyrian sorcery was rooted in blood or fire. The sorcerers of the Freehold could see across mountains, seas, and deserts with one of these glass candles.
note: Glass candles confirmed as magical Valyrian artifacts housed in Citadel

verbatim_quote: I swore to give you Westeros, and here is your first taste. A morsel, nothing more . . . but we shall feast before the fall of night!
note: Euron's ship is called Silence; he silences his crew; power based on fear and silence, opposite of Song (from 'A Song of Ice and Fire')

verbatim_quote: The Boltons have always been as cruel as they were cunning, but this one seems a beast in human skin, said Glover.
note: Ramsay explicitly described as beast in human skin

verbatim_quote: Robett Glover took up the tale. We may never know all that happened at Winterfell, when Ser Rodrik Cassel tried to take the castle back from Theon Greyjoy's ironmen.
note: Robett Glover present at meeting with Davos and Manderly, recounting Winterfell events

verbatim_quote: She don't speak, said the big man in the yellow cloak. You bloody bastards cut her throat too deep for that. But she remembers.
note: Lady Stoneheart (resurrected Catelyn) cannot speak due to throat wound but remembers the Red Wedding

verbatim_quote: He is no lord, said Alys scornfully. My brother Harry is the rightful lord, and by law I am his heir. A daughter comes before an uncle. Uncle Arnolf is only castellan. He's my great-uncle, actually, my father's uncle. Cregan is his son. I suppose that makes him a cousin, but we always called him uncle. Now they mean to make me call him husband.
note: Alys Karstark confirms Arnolf's forced marriage scheme to seize lordship; conspiracy against her brother Harry

verbatim_quote: He was on his way to Riverrun when . . . when he heard about Lyanna, and went to King's Landing instead. It was a rash thing to do. The gallant fool, was what he called Brandon.
note: Brandon characterized as gallant fool and rash—personality consistent with jousting in disguise

Tried grep patterns: 'Brandon tall', 'tall Brandon' across all AGOT chapters; no direct description of Brandon Stark being tall found in book corpus. Brandon appears as 'remembered Brandon's laughter' in agot-eddard-15.md line 41 but no explicit height description. Cannot ground specific claim that Brandon was tall.

verbatim_quote: All I did, I did for House Lannister. Always . . . for years . . . your lord father, ask him, I was ever his true servant . . . 'twas I who bid Aerys open his gates . . .
note: Pycelle's confession establishes Lannister loyalty as his primary motivation, not maester conspiracy
